chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-931 of 2021 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Before; Mr. Justice Arshad Hussain Khan Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah 1. For orders on MA-927/2021. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For hearing of main case. 11.02.2021. Petitioners through their attorney Manzoor Hussain. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional A.G. = Urgency granted. It is stated on behalf of the petitioners by their attorney that they are employees of the Sindh Public Service Commission and are being discriminated by their officers, therefore, they are intending to form a welfare association to unearth malpractice in Sindh Public Service Commission. By stating so, a direction was sought by them against Chairman Sindh Public Service Commission to issue NOC in their favour to form welfare association. Learned A.A.G has sought for dismissal of the instant petition by contending that it is misconceived. We have considered the above arguments and perused the record. Apparently, the petitioners without waiting for the decision to be taken by the Sindh Public Service Commission on their application for issuance of no objection certificate have come before this Court with prayer to direct the Chairman, Sindh Public Service Commission 2 to issue no objection certificate in their favour to form a welfare association. Such direction if, issued by this Court without having look of the decision to be taken by Sindh Public Service Commission would amount to making interference of an Institution’s internal affairs; therefore, same could hardly be issued by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction.

Apparently, the petitioners without waiting for the decision to be taken by the Sindh Public Service Commission on their application for issuance of no objection certificate have come before this Court with prayer to direct the Chairman, Sindh Public Service Commission 2 to issue no objection certificate in their favour to form a welfare association. Such direction if, issued by this Court without having look of the decision to be taken by Sindh Public Service Commission would amount to making interference of an Institution’s internal affairs; therefore, same could hardly be issued by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction. Consequently, the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Ahmed/Pa,
